title: ALGM Q1 Results: Data Center and Auto Drive Growth Despite Margin Pressures
text: Allegro MicroSystems delivered Q1 CY2026 revenue of $243.2 million, exceeding analyst expectations by 3.2% with robust 26.1% year-over-year growth. The chip designer's adjusted EBITDA came in at $49.69 million, representing a 20.4% margin. Management guided Q2 revenue at $250 million midpoint, surpassing analyst estimates of $245.4 million, with GAAP EPS guidance of $0.21. Allegro experienced notable growth in data center-related sales, driven by increased adoption of high-efficiency fan drivers and current sensors. The company is capitalizing on expanding content opportunities as AI infrastructure evolves toward higher power densities. Electric vehicle and advanced driver assistance applications propelled automotive segment gains. Management highlighted design wins in steering, braking, and high-voltage traction inverters. Industrial and robotics sales doubled year-over-year, supported by design wins with leading robotics manufacturers in China. Despite revenue strength, gross margins faced pressure from commodity price increases. Management aims to improve profitability through operational efficiencies and selective price increases.
